teamObjective.explanation: Because the game is at the opening lead and our hand is weak in non-trump clubs, leading the lowest card best supports the partner's likely goal of shedding cards while preserving our stronger cards for later tricks.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2) holds 3 cards and has made no public plays. At this early stage they likely want a low, manageable lead that they can beat with a higher non-trump or trump, allowing them to shed a card and move toward going out.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents have not acted publicly. Player 1 holds only 2 cards, placing them near finish, so they likely want to seize control or finish quickly; player 3 has 3 cards and may hold counters to stop our team.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Because the game is at the opening lead and our hand is weak in non-trump clubs, leading the lowest card best supports the partner's likely goal of shedding cards while preserving our stronger cards for later tricks.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leading the 6 uses our strongest non-trump. It might win the trick outright, but it spends our best card early and reduces the chance the partner can beat the lead to reduce their hand.
